title: Neottia cernua
intro: Neottia cernua, also known as the nodding ladies' tresses, is a perennial orchid species native to North America. This plant has been utilized in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Neottia cernua include iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Neottia cernua is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in combination with other herbs to make cough syrups and respiratory remedies.
taxonomy: Neottia cernua is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. This plant belongs to the genus Neottia, which is a group of terrestrial orchids commonly known as the "bee orchids". Neottia cernua is further categorized as Neottia cernua within the species. The classification is based on the plant's morphological characteristics and genetic relationships. At the species level, Neottia cernua is often placed within the subsection Orchidaceae subsect. Platantherinae.
